Hydrangea quercifolia, commonly known as the oakleaf hydrangea, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
